details: The "Aapki Beti Yojana" scheme was launched by the School Education Department, Government of Rajasthan, to support girls in continuing their education in government schools from Class 1 to 12. Under the scheme, financial assistance of ₹2,100/- per year is provided to girls studying in Class 1 to 8 in government schools who belong to families living below the poverty line (BPL) and whose one or both parents have passed away. For girls studying in Class 9 to 12, the assistance is ₹2,500/- per year. The scheme is implemented by the Rajasthan Balika Shiksha Foundation.
benefits: ₹2,100/- per annum for girl students in classes 1
₹2,500/- per annum for girl students in classes 9
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to the bank account linked with Jan Aadhaar of the family head or the girl child.
eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
The applicant should be a girl student
The applicant should be studying in a government school in Rajasthan.
The applicant should be studying in classes 1
The applicant’s family should fall under the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.
The applicant should have lost either one parent (single parent) or both parents.

online: To avail of the benefits of the scheme, the parents/guardians need to contact their respective government school.
Data Entry on Shala Darpan Portal:
School authorities log in through the
Navigate to the "Student" tab and fill required details in Form-9.
Under the “Mandatory Student Information for Beneficiary Schemes” section, select class-wise eligible girls under “BPL” and “Orphan/Single Parent” categories.
Visit the Beneficiary Scheme Portal through School Login.
Select the appropriate scheme and approve and lock the displayed list of eligible girls.
Locked data is reflected on the DEO (primary/secondary) office login.
DEO completes final verification using OTP-based approval.
Before submission, ensure that the girl’s Jan Aadhaar is verified on the Shala Darpan Portal, as funds are transferred via DBT.

documents_required: Passport size photo of the applicant
Jan Aadhaar Card of Family Head or the Girl
Bank Account Passbook linked with Jan Aadhaar

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Girls from BPL families studying in government schools (classes 1 st to 12 th ) who have lost one or both parents are eligible.

faqs.question: What is the amount of financial aid provided?
faqs.answer: ₹2,100 for classes 1 st to 8 th and ₹2,500 for classes 9 th to 12 th annually.

faqs.question: How is the amount disbursed?
faqs.answer: The amount is transferred directly to the Jan Aadhaar-linked bank account of the family head or the girl.

faqs.question: Do students studying in private schools qualify for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, only students studying in government schools are eligible.

faqs.question: Is re-application required every year?
faqs.answer: Yes, the application must be submitted each academic year.

faqs.question: Can a student with an unverified Jan Aadhaar apply?
faqs.answer: No, Jan Aadhaar must be verified for the application to be processed.

faqs.question: How does the school submit data on the portal?
faqs.answer: Schools use Shala Darpan Login and follow the structured steps to submit Form-9 and approve beneficiaries.

faqs.question: What is the role of the Beneficiary Scheme Portal?
faqs.answer: It is used for viewing, approving, locking, and submitting the beneficiary list.

faqs.question: Is a death certificate mandatory?
faqs.answer: Yes, the death certificate of the deceased parent(s) is required.

faqs.question: Can Jan Aadhaar be in the name of the girl herself?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Jan Aadhaar can be in the name of the girl or the family head.
